title: Event
description:
One event within a run (join-spine `run_id` row: run â†” events â†”
manifest). `detail` is deliberately open (`serde_json::Value`) â€”
events are the most heterogeneous record kind (tool calls, token
deltas, tool errors, â€¦); narrowing `detail` to a closed shape is a
later, per-event-family change, not S1's.

schema.description:
Per-kind schema version, bumped on any breaking field change to
that kind (design D2; `canon fmt`/`canon migrate`, S11, key off
it).

Actor.description:
Who/what produced a record â€” structured, never a bare `by: String`
(design D2, the donor audit's biggest cross-family gap).

`role` is `Option` (S11 design D5, "actor backfill is best-effort
from adjacent fields; absent stays absent"): a migrated legacy
record whose only source field is a bare `by: "legacy-ci-machine"`
string carries no role information at all â€” `canon migrate` maps
that string to `agent_id` and leaves `role: null` rather than
guessing, so the field must be able to express "unknown", not just
"known". Every record canon itself originates still supplies a role
via [`Actor::new`]; only backfilled/migrated records use
[`Actor::new_unattributed`].

RecordKind.description:
The thirteen closed record kinds `canon-model` recognizes (design D1;
`Subject` is the reviewed 13th kind, added by s36 â€” the "a new kind is
a reviewed, breaking `canon-model` change" process design D1 mandates,
exercised for real). A fourteenth kind is again a reviewed, breaking
change â€” never a `kind: String` escape hatch (design D1's explicitly
rejected alternative: an untyped `payload: serde_json::Value` would
silently recreate the "no documented join key" problem inside canon
itself).

RoleId.description: kebab-case role slug (`[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`), e.g. `implementer`, `reviewer` â€” joins: not a join-spine key; used by `Actor.role` and `regime_key`'s `<role>` segment

RunId.description: ULID (Crockford base32, 26 chars) â€” joins: run â†” events â†” manifest

SessionId.description: opaque, non-empty agent-CLI session token; derivation source varies by adapter (Claude Code/Codex: transcript filename stem; omp/pi: in-file header `id` field) â€” this type validates shape only, never a single fixed format â€” joins: session â†” cost â†” run â†” trajectory
